entities.name: ARACHIS GLABRATA
entities.description: A peanut species belonging to section Rhizomatosae that possesses multiple disease resistance.

entities.name: ARACHIS HYPOGAEA
entities.description: The cultivated peanut species used as a target for gene introgression.

entities.name: ARACHIS DURANENSIS
entities.description: A wild peanut species used as a target for gene introgression.

entities.name: ARACHIS DIOGOI
entities.description: A wild peanut species used as a target for gene introgression.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist diseases, which is a target for transfer from A. glabrata.

entities.name: ISOZYME ANALYSIS
entities.description: A biochemical method used to analyze the introgression of DNA from A. glabrata.

entities.name: RAPD ANALYSIS
entities.description: Random Amplified Polymorphic DNA analysis used to analyze DNA fragment patterns and introgression.

entities.name: GENE INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The process of incorporating genetic material from one species into the genome of another.

entities.name: BACKCROSSING
entities.description: The process of crossing a hybrid with one of its parents to obtain BC1 seeds and recover the recurrent parent genome.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Specific genes responsible for disease resistance transferred from A. glabrata to other peanut species.
